title: grants — developer configuration
description: Developer / operator configuration. Mirrors `config/grants.php`. Static per deploy; changing requires redeploy + `config:cache`. Distinct from settings.json (tenant-editable at runtime).

keys.description: Default `expires_at` = now() + this many days when create call doesn't specify. Callers may override per-grant.
Hard ceiling on non-permanent grants. Enterprise tenants with `permanent_grants` entitlement can set expires_at=NULL to bypass; other tiers are capped here.
How many hours before expiry the NotifyGrantExpiringJob dispatches the warning notification.
Whether the `reason` field is required. Should always be true — audit trail promise. Disabling requires an ADR.
Minimum characters in `reason` after trim. 20 is the compromise between 'meaningful sentence' and 'not so long it invites laziness like copy-pasting Lorem Ipsum'.
Case-insensitive placeholder patterns refused by the ValidGrantReason rule. Update to broaden the guard against low-effort reasons.
Hard cap on the permissions[] array length. 64 is generous; typical grants have 1-5 permissions.
L1 in-process evaluation cache TTL. 5 minutes is a compromise between staleness (a revoked grant taking effect) and cache-warm-cost (rebuilding on every request).
L2 Redis evaluation cache TTL. 1 hour — invalidation events do the heavy lifting, TTL is a safety net.
Redis key prefix for the evaluation cache.
Toggle L2 Redis cache participation. Off = L1 only + DB fallback.
Whether the resolver short-circuits on the first deny. Should always be true — this is the safety guarantee we sell. Disabling requires an ADR.
How many days to retain revoked allow grants before hard-delete.
How many days to retain expired allow grants before hard-delete.
Retention policy for terminal deny grants. Value ALWAYS 'indefinite' — enforced by policy, not config. Present here for documentation.
Hard cap on CSV batch size. Larger uploads refused with GRANT_BULK_TOO_LARGE.
How long a bulk-batch job stays retryable after failure.
Max grants pre-loaded per request by the warm-cache middleware. Beyond this, resolver falls back to lazy L2 lookups.
How many top-recent grants GrantCacheWarmerHook pre-loads on tenant init.
Fraction of GrantEvaluationCacheHit events actually fired (sampling to reduce volume). 1.0 = all, 0.0 = none. Miss events fire 100% — every miss is interesting.
Per-tenant alert threshold. Above this, on-call is paged (privilege-escalation stampede).
Enable manual admin CRUD path.
Enable the access-request → grant bridge listener.
Enable the delegation-driven grant path. Reserved for access/delegation module — no-op until that module lands.
Enable the CSV bulk-issue path. Complementary to the bulk_grant_provision entitlement.
